# .agent Community

> The .agent domain is the proposed AI-native TLD for autonomous agents. Join the community driving the ICANN application.

## Pages

- [About the .agent Community](https://agentcommunity.org/about.md): Learn why we are applying for the .agent TLD to keep the agentic web open, trustworthy, and human-centered.
- [Frequently Asked Questions](https://agentcommunity.org/faq.md): Common questions about the .agent domain, the ICANN application, pre-registration, community governance, cryptocurrency, and what the agentic web actually is.
- [Glossary](https://agentcommunity.org/glossary.md): Definitions for the agentic web: .agent TLD, Agent Identity & Discovery (AID), Community Priority Evaluation (CPE), agent naming taxonomy, and more.
- [Pricing](https://agentcommunity.org/pricing.md): Agent Community pricing. It's free. That's it. That's the page.
- [Privacy Policy](https://agentcommunity.org/privacy.md): How agentcommunity.org collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal information.
- [Terms of Service](https://agentcommunity.org/terms.md): Terms of service for agentcommunity.org, the .agent domain community platform.
- [Why Join the Agent Community?](https://agentcommunity.org/why-join.md): Discover the benefits of joining the Agent Community. Access OSS tools, collaborative networks, and help shape the future of the agentic web with open standards.

## Documentation

- [Get Involved](https://agentcommunity.org/docs/get-involved.md): How to join the .agent community — contribute to specs, join working groups, reserve your name.
- [Governance](https://agentcommunity.org/docs/governance.md): How the .agent community will be governed — charter, principles, and what's coming.
- [.agent Community](https://agentcommunity.org/docs.md): Open specs, governance, and infrastructure for the agentic web. Community-governed, not corporate-controlled.
- [One-pager](https://agentcommunity.org/docs/one-pager.md): A one-page overview of the Agent Community, what we're building, and how to get involved.
- [Why .agent?](https://agentcommunity.org/docs/why-agent.md): Why agents need human-readable names and why the naming layer must be community-governed.
- [Work Items](https://agentcommunity.org/docs/work-items.md): Specifications and protocols the .agent community is developing.

## Blog

- [Missing record](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/missing_record.md): The MX-like record for agents: AID’s DNS TXT points any domain to its agent (MCP, A2A, or OpenAPI) for zero-friction discovery.
- [What Should Happen When You Type a Domain Name?](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/what_when_domain.md): Typing a domain should just connect: AID adds a DNS-based address book so apps discover an agent’s URI and protocol automatically.
- [Finding door a2a](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/finding_door_a2a.md): Use DNS to find an agent’s front door: AID’s TXT record points to an A2A AgentCard so clients can discover and connect cleanly.
- [Missing grep mcp](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/missing_grep_mcp.md): The discovery layer MCP forgot: AID uses a DNS TXT record to map domains to MCP endpoints so hosts can auto-connect.
- [Door or Address book](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/door_or_address_book.md): How does an AI agent know what to do when it hits a locked door on the web?
- [Web Bot Auth, not for agents](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/web_auth_box_not_for_agents.md): signed interactions, or a product moat wrapped in a standard?
- [Identity joins discovery - from v1 to v1.1](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/identity-joins-discovery.md): Moving from discovery to trusted discovery
- [Agent Identity & Discovery](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/agent-identity-and-discovery.md): identity for the agentic web
- [AgentBrief: a curation agent for the agentic web](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/agent-brief-agentic-news.md): A workflow-first newsroom built on taste, signal, and the agentic web
- [Add AID to ERC-8004: keep discovery fast, keep identity portable](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/aid-to-8004.md): Why ERC-8004 needs a DNS-first discovery layer to stay fresh and portable
- [Why AID Now Supports UCP](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/why-aid-now-supports-ucp.md): Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) is now supported in AID, enabling agents to discover and transact with commerce endpoints via DNS.
- [WebMCP and AID: Browser-first tools meet Internet-first discovery](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/webmcp-and-aid.md): Google’s WebMCP makes websites agent-ready inside the browser. AID stays DNS-native so agents can discover endpoints from any domain, with or without a website.
- [Code Mode and AID: Massive tool scale, clean discovery](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/codemode-and-aid.md): Code Mode makes large APIs practical for agents by compressing tool surface area. AID stays focused on DNS-first discovery, so protocol routing remains simple and interoperable.
- [Code is State](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/code-is-state.md): Every variable in your program is mutable state. Your source code is not. That deal is off. We now have systems that rewrite their own code as a normal part of operation.
- [PKA as External Trust Anchor](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/external_identity_anchor.md): How AID PKA addresses the cross-boundary agent authentication problem — and what questions remain open.
- [Bootstrapping ARDP via AID: A Non-Normative Interop Mapping](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/bootstrapping-ardp-via-aid.md): How AID DNS records can serve as a lightweight bootstrap layer for ARDP agent resolution and discovery. An optional, illustrative mapping for deployments where the ARDP authority is a DNS name.
- [AID is now an IETF Internet-Draft](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/aid-ietf-internet-draft.md): We submitted draft-nemethi-aid-agent-identity-discovery-00 to the IETF Datatracker on March 16, 2026, and filed for IANA registration of _agent.
- [Why AID ships on TXT records](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/why-txt-records.md): We like SVCB. We plan to use it. But a third of DNS providers can't publish one yet, and agent adoption isn't waiting for them.
- [From 3,000 to 21,000: what we shipped this week](https://agentcommunity.org/blog/scaling-week.md): Brave endorsed the Agent Community on Tuesday. Membership surged 7x. Here is what we did behind the scenes to keep everything running.
